mainText: What you have given specialness has left you bankrupt, and your treasure house barren and empty, with an open door inviting everything that would disturb your peace to enter and destroy. Long ago we said consider not the means by which salvation is attained, nor how to reach it. But DO consider, and consider well, whether it is your wish that you might see your brother sinless. To specialness the answer must be “no.” A sinless brother IS its enemy, while sin, if it were possible, would be its friend.Your brother’s sins would justify itself, and give it meaning that the truth denies.All that is real proclaims his sinlessness. All that is false proclaims his sins as real. If HE is sinful, then is YOUR reality not real, but just a dream of specialness which lasts an instant, crumbling into dust. Do not defend this senseless dream, in which God is bereft of what He loves, and you remain beyond salvation. Only this is certain in this shifting world which has no meaning in reality:When peace is not with you entirely, and when you suffer pain of any kind, you have beheld some sin within your brother, and have REJOICED at what you thought was there.Your specialness seemed safe because of it. And thus you saved what YOU appointed to be your savior, and crucified the one whom God has given you instead.
